steps.title: Check current jaeger deployment
steps.description:
Verify your jaeger version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n jaeger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
helm list -n jaeger 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working jaeger installation.

steps.title: Review jaeger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant jaeger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n jaeger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get configmap -n jaeger -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=jaeger
```
Since 7.0 just came out of Elasticsearch, how long will it typically take for Jaeger Ingest to be updated to 7.0?

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support Elasticsearch 7.x
steps.description:
Adds support for Elasticsearch 7.x
Depends on https://github.com/olivere/elastic/pull/1146

- Updates github.com/olivere/elastic to 6.2.21
- Removes the deprecated _default_ field from mappings
- Replaces document types with '_doc' as a transition step to removing them entirely
- Sets
```yaml
HTTP Error: Search service failed: json: cannot unmarshal object into Go struct field SearchHits.total of type int64
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n jaeger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger
kubectl get events -n jaeger --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support Elasticsearch 7.x" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Adds support for Elasticsearch 7.x
Depends on https://github.com/olivere/elastic/pull/1146

- Updates github.com/olivere/elastic to 6.2.21
- Removes the deprecated _default_ field from mappings
- Replaces document types with '_doc' as a transition step to removing them entirely
- Sets include_type_name for compatibility between elasticsearch 6.x and 7.x

rest_total_hits_as_int support is also
codeSnippets: HTTP Error: Search service failed: json: cannot unmarshal object into Go struct field SearchHits.total of type int64

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with jaeger installed or the issue environment reproducible.
